source: 82-year-old gentleman from an assisted living facility, history of atrial fibrillation on apixaban, BPH, and mild cognitive impairment, admitted for community-acquired pneumonia. He's had four days of productive cough, low-grade fevers, and decreased activity. Staff at the facility noted he was eating less. On exam temp 38.1, heart rate is irregular at 96, blood pressure 134 over 78, satting 93 on 2 liters nasal cannula. He has left lower lobe crackles and egophony. Chest x-ray confirms a left lower lobe consolidation. White count is 14.2, he's not hypotensive, lactate normal at 1.4. CURB-65 is intermediate so admitting to the floor, not ICU. Starting ceftriaxone and azithromycin for CAP. Continue his apixaban for the atrial fibrillation, his rate is controlled. Continue tamsulosin. Encourage oral intake, IV fluids for now, monitor. Speech therapy to evaluate swallowing given the decreased intake and his baseline cognitive impairment.
reference:
HPI: 82M from assisted living with atrial fibrillation (on apixaban), BPH, and mild cognitive impairment, admitted for community-acquired pneumonia — 4 days of productive cough, low-grade fevers, decreased activity and oral intake per facility staff.

Exam: T 38.1, HR 96 irregular, BP 134/78, SpO2 93% on 2L NC. Left lower lobe crackles with egophony.
Data: CXR left lower lobe consolidation. WBC 14.2. Lactate 1.4 (normal), normotensive.

Assessment/Plan:
# Community-acquired pneumonia, CURB-65 intermediate — admit to floor (not ICU). Ceftriaxone + azithromycin. IV fluids, encourage PO intake, monitor.
# Atrial fibrillation, rate-controlled — continue apixaba­n.
# BPH — continue tamsulosin.
# Decreased intake / mild cognitive impairment — speech therapy to evaluate swallowing.
Admit to hospital medicine.
